items.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6, Now Preferred Model for Microsoft 365 Copilot
items.summary: OpenAI has officially launched its new family of models with GPT-5.6, which is immediately being integrated as the preferred model for Microsoft 365 Copilot, signaling a deepening partnership despite recent 'breakup chatter'.

items.title: Fidji Simo Steps Down from OpenAI's No. 2 Role
items.summary: Fidji Simo, a prominent figure, has resigned from her position as OpenAI's second-in-command, marking a significant leadership change within the leading AI research company.

items.title: Railway Secures $100 Million to Challenge AWS with AI-Native Cloud Infrastructure
items.summary: Railway has raised $100 million in funding to develop an AI-native cloud infrastructure platform, aiming to compete directly with established giants like AWS.

items.title: Google Redesigns Search Box for First Time in 25 Years, Signaling AI Integration
items.summary: Google has unveiled the first redesign of its iconic search box in 25 years, a move that is expected to significantly impact user interaction and likely reflects deeper AI integration.

items.title: Accurate, Interdisciplinary and Transparent Structure-property Understanding with Deep Native Structural Reasoning
items.summary: New research introduces a deep learning approach for accurate, interdisciplinary, and transparent understanding of structure-property relationships, leveraging native structural reasoning.

items.title: Dual Latent Memory in Vision-Language-Action Models for Robotic Manipulation
items.summary: Researchers propose a novel architecture incorporating dual latent memory into Vision-Language-Action models to enhance robotic manipulation capabilities.

items.title: Vidu S1: A Real-Time Interactive Video Generation Model
items.summary: A new model named Vidu S1 has been introduced, capable of generating real-time interactive video, pushing the boundaries of dynamic content creation.

items.title: Scaling Mixture-of-Experts Video Pretraining for Embodied Intelligence
items.summary: New research explores scaling Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models for video pretraining to significantly advance embodied intelligence systems.

items.title: Claude Code Costs Up to $200/Month While Goose Offers Similar Functionality for Free
items.summary: A new report highlights a significant cost disparity, noting that Claude Code charges up to $200 monthly for services that a competitor, Goose, provides for free.

items.title: ChatGPT Positioned as a Partner for Ambitious Work
items.summary: OpenAI is marketing ChatGPT as an essential partner for users undertaking their most ambitious projects, emphasizing its enhanced capabilities and reliability.

items.title: Stratagems #9: AI Suppliers Fight, Logs Show Neither Was Clean
items.summary: An investigative report reveals a conflict between two AI suppliers where analysis of their logs indicated questionable practices from both parties.

items.title: LLM-as-a-Tutor: Policy-Aware Prompt Adaptation for Non-Verifiable RL
items.summary: Researchers introduce 'LLM-as-a-Tutor,' a method that uses Large Language Models for policy-aware prompt adaptation to improve non-verifiable Reinforcement Learning.

items.title: Your Hand-Typed Slop Isn't Honest. It's Just Slower.
items.summary: An opinion piece argues that manual coding, often seen as more 'honest,' is simply less efficient than leveraging AI tools, suggesting a shift in developer mindset.

items.title: I Deleted 200 Lines of Code I Didn't Write and Learned More Than When I Wrote It...
items.summary: A developer shares an experience of gaining significant insights by refactoring and deleting AI-generated code, highlighting the learning potential in code review over initial creation.

items.title: GPT-5.5 Bio Bug Bounty Program Launched by OpenAI
items.summary: OpenAI has initiated a bug bounty program specifically for its GPT-5.5 Bio model, inviting researchers to identify and report vulnerabilities related to biological applications.
